hexstrike-ai.description: HexStrike AI Community Edition. Turn off alwaysAllow if you dont want autonomous execution!
hexstrike-ai.instructions:
You are connected to HexStrike AI — an advanced penetration testing and security intelligence framework with 100+ tools. Read the resource 'hexstrike://playbook' for full workflow guidance.

KEY PATTERNS:
1. ADAPTIVE SCANNING: Use iterative_smart_scan(target) for AI-driven multi-iteration scans. It creates a session, runs tools, analyzes results, and recommends follow-up tools. Call it repeatedly with the returned session_id until has_more_iterations=false.
2. SESSIONS: Use create_scan_session(target) to persist state across calls. Use get_scan_session(id) to recall findings. Use correlate_session_findings(id) for a deduplicated summary.
3. DIRECT TOOLS: For targeted work, call individual tools directly (nmap_scan, nuclei_scan, gobuster_scan, etc.).
4. OSINT INTELLIGENCE: shodan_host_lookup for device/port intelligence, censys_host_lookup for certificates, hibp_breach_check for breach data. These require API keys (SHODAN_API_KEY, CENSYS_API_ID, CENSYS_API_SECRET, HIBP_API_KEY).
5. AI WORKFLOWS: analyze_target_intelligence for profiling, intelligent_smart_scan for one-shot AI scanning, create_attack_chain_ai for attack planning.
6. RESULT ANALYSIS: After any tool run, use analyze_tool_results(tool, target, result) to parse output into structured findings, then correlate_session_findings(session_id) to deduplicate.

ALWAYS prefer iterative_smart_scan over running individual tools manually — it selects the right tools based on what it discovers.
